name: Emirati Arabic

ancestors.notes: Gulf Arabic base — inherits *q→[ɡ], velar affrication GULF_K_AFFRICATION/GULF_G_AFFRICATION, interdental retention and emphatic spreading.

notes: Emirati Arabic (إماراتي) — UAE: Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Sharjah, RAK. Inherits the Gulf features: *q → [ɡ]; velar affrication /k/→[tʃ], /ɡ/→[dʒ] before high front /i, iː/; interdentals /θ ð ðˤ/ retained. Emirati-specific: (a) jiim and the reflex of *q vary with the palatal glide [j] (grapheme ج → [dʒ]~[j]; ɡ → [ɡ]~[j]) — Szreder & Derrick (2023) find the [dʒ~j] alternation to be a live synchronic process, and that the presence of a nearby similar coronal obstruent inhibits the (voiced) [j] variant of this dʒ~j alternation; (b) the /k~tʃ/ alternation is, per Szreder & Derrick (2023), a COMPLETED phonemic change (now two separate phonemes), so the inherited GULF_K_AFFRICATION rule is a predictive approximation of the historical distribution, not a synchronic process — and, unlike the voiced target, /k/-affrication was NOT inhibited by nearby similar consonants (if anything the reverse); (c) heaviest English influence of the Gulf dialects (Dubai effect): /p/, /v/, /tʃ/, /ŋ/ thoroughly integrated; strong South-Asian community lexical influence. Interior/tribal varieties (RAK, Fujairah) are more conservative. Input contract: fully diacritised (tashkeel) Arabic.

sources.author: Szreder, M. & Derrick, D.
sources.title: Phonological conditioning of affricate variability in Emirati Arabic
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association
sources.notes: Abstract and published summary read (full text paywalled — cited only for what was read). Emirati-specific: [k~tʃ] is a completed phonemic change (lexicalised); [dʒ~j] is a synchronic phonological process; a nearby similar coronal obstruent inhibits the (voiced) [j] variant of the dʒ~j alternation, whereas /k/-affrication to [tʃ] is not inhibited by similar consonants.

sources.author: Alshammari, K. K.
sources.title: An Optimality-Theoretic Account of Velar Affrication in Northern Najdi Arabic
sources.publisher: Journal of Language Teaching and Research 17(4)
sources.notes: Read in full. Literature review (p.1335) of Gulf velar affrication (Mustafawi Qatari, Matar Kuwaiti, Johnstone 1967 lexicalisation) supporting the inherited GULF_K/G_AFFRICATION rules.

location.notes: Abu Dhabi. A national variety within the Gulf continuum: the capital stands in for the country. [Wikidata Q1519 P625]
